milestones.title: Born in Kaišiadorys
milestones.body: A small town in central Lithuania - quiet streets, the sound of trains at dusk. Every story needs a starting point; this one starts here.

milestones.title: First computer at age 3
milestones.body: A beige box in the corner of the room. I was small enough that the keyboard felt enormous, but the curiosity stuck - and hasn't left.

image.alt: Young Lukas with his first computer

milestones.title: Moved to Kaunas
milestones.body: A bigger city, bigger sky. New streets to learn, new windows to look out of. Kaunas quickly stopped feeling new.

milestones.title: First websites at age 11
milestones.body: HTML by view-source, one more <div> at midnight. No tutorials, just patience and the refresh button. That's where it clicked.

milestones.title: Game servers and Linux
milestones.body: Counter-Strike, Team Fortress 2, Minecraft, San-Andreas. Hosting for friends, breaking things for fun. Linux started as a side effect and became the point.

milestones.title: Admin at Kiberzona
milestones.body: The biggest gaming community in Lithuania. Thousands of players, a long education in how online people actually behave. Creating websites, hosting servers.

milestones.title: Competitive Team Fortress 2
milestones.body: Top level player in Europe, representing national Lithuanian team, captain of national team, many teammates who became lifelong friends.

milestones.title: Got married
milestones.body: The best teammate I've ever had, now officially on the roster for good.

milestones.title: Son Rokas was born
milestones.body: A small human joined the team. Suddenly the scale of everything changes - and the best project on the list has no deadline.
